explanation: The structured NL property captures the input natural language by stating that "whenever the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good, the autopilot shall change states from MANEUVER to STANDBY".  The "whenever" decision is used to express the condition of the pilot being in control and sensor data being good. The decision1_substring represents "whenever the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good", while the decision3_substring represents "the autopilot shall change states from MANEUVER to STANDBY".  Since the input natural language specifies that the autopilot should change states "when" the conditions are met,  the "at the next timepoint" decision is used to express the change in states.
decision3: at the next timepoint satisfy bool_exp3
decision1_substring: whenever the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good
decision3_substring: the autopilot shall change states from MANEUVER to STANDBY
output_structured_natural_language: whenever standby & good, at the next timepoint satisfy state_is_STANDBY
